items.title: GS Gill Sports, long-time Adidas retailer, to shut landmark outlet
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: GS Gill Sports, a retailer that predates Malaysia's independence, is set to close its outlet on Jalan Tuanku Abdul Rahman and move its operations online. Others remembered GS Gill as a trusted destination for authentic Adidas products long before online shopping and international sports retailers became commonplace. One Threads user said sports enthusiasts from the pre-e-commerce era would undoubtedly remember GS Gill, describing it as the company that first introduced Adidas to Malaysia.

items.title: Johor election key contests — Machap, Puteri Wangsa, Bukit Kepong in spotlight
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 27 — Several high-profile contests are set to dominate the campaign for the 16th Johor state election, with heavyweight politicians, former ministers, and closely watched constituencies expected to shape the outcome when voters head to the polls on July 11. Topping the list is Machap, where Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi of Barisan Nasional (BN) faces a straight fight against Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) Nur Hafiz Roslan, a lawyer. Another key battleground is Puteri Wangsa, where former education minister Dr Maszlee Malik of PH faces the election’s highest-profile five-cornered contest against BN’s Teow Chia Ling, Nicholas Paul Vincent of Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama), Rashifa Al Junied of the Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (MUDA), and Independent Wang Wee Siong.

items.title: Civil service pension revamp crucial to protect nation's fiscal health, say economists
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government's proposal for a new retirement scheme for civil servants is a necessary structural reform, economists said. They said the reform is crucial to prevent soaring civil service pension liabilities from crippling the nation's fiscal health. Anwar said the proposed mechanism for the civil service retirement scheme, known as the "contributory permanent appointment" scheme, is still being studied.

items.title: Diesel subsidies extended to small traders with company vehicles
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Small traders with company-registered jeeps and pickup trucks will soon enjoy diesel subsidies. Previously, diesel subsidy eligibility was limited to vehicles registered under an individual's name through the BUDI Diesel programme and company-registered goods transport vehicles. Armizan said the government will also review and adjust the fixed diesel quota for several categories of commercial land transport vehicles under SKDS.

items.title: Jalaluddin: Umno proposed PKR, Amanah leaders to replace Aminuddin as MB
items.summary: JELEBU: Negri Sembilan Umno chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias today claimed Barisan Nasional (BN) had proposed two Pakatan Harapan (PH) leaders to replace Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun as menteri besar after the party withdrew its support for him during the state's political crisis. Jalaluddin said the proposed candidates were Pilah assemblyman Datuk Noorzunita Begum Mohd Ibrahim, who is also a PKR state executive councillor, and Klawang assemblyman Datuk Bakri Sawir of Amanah. The BN Negri Sembilan deputy chairman said he proposed the two names to what he described as a "key figure in Putrajaya", who contacted him after Umno withdrew its support for Aminuddin on April 27.

items.title: Malaysia to review diesel quota rates for commercial vehicles by August
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 27 — The government will review the fixed quota rates of subsidised diesel for several types of land transport vehicles for goods under the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) to ensure that the subsidy distribution is more accurate and in line with actual operational needs. Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the decision was made following yesterday’s Cabinet Meeting which discussed the status of progress in implementing targeted fuel subsidy through the BUDI Diesel, BUDI95, Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) and Subsidised Petrol Control System (SKPS) mechanisms. He said previously, the eligibility conditions for diesel subsidies were limited to vehicles registered in the name of individuals for BUDI Diesel and business vehicles or goods transport companies under the class or code of use for consumer goods transport for SKDS.

items.title: Govt to review fixed quota for SKDS subsidised diesel
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The government will review the fixed quota rates of subsidised diesel for several types of land transport vehicles for goods under the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) to ensure that the subsidy distribution is more accurate and in line with actual operational needs. Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the decision was made following yesterday's Cabinet Meeting which discussed the status of progress in implementing targeted fuel subsidy through the Budi Diesel, BUDI95, Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) and Subsidised Petrol Control System (SKPS) mechanisms. He said previously, the eligibility conditions for diesel subsidies were limited to vehicles registered in the name of individuals for Budi Diesel and business vehicles or goods transport companies under the class or code of use for consumer goods transport for SKDS.

items.title: Faris Johari installed as new Undang of Sungei Ujong, Hassan as Undang of Rembau
items.summary: KUALA PILAH: The Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, today formalised the installation of two new Undang (ruling chieftains) in separate ceremonies at Istana Besar Seri Menanti, underscoring the continued importance of adat and consensus in the state's governance structure. In the morning ceremony, Muhammad Faris Johari, 28, was installed as the 11th Undang of Luak Sungei Ujong, bearing the title Datuk Klana Petra, while in the afternoon, Hassan Ab Hamid, 67, was installed as the 22nd Undang of Luak Rembau, with the title Datuk Sedia Raja. Also in attendance were Tunku Ampuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Aishah Rohani Tengku Besar Mahmud, Tunku Besar Seri Menanti Tunku Ali Redhauddin Tuanku Muhriz, and Tunku Panglima Besar Tunku Zain Al-'Abidin.

items.title: Indonesian man walks across 17 countries to fulfil dream of praying in the Holy Land
items.summary: Vico said his expedition began with a single purpose — to perform prayers at Masjidil Haram in Mecca and Masjid Nabawi in Madinah through a journey driven by faith and perseverance. "I reached Mecca on Jan 26 after walking through 17 countries. Despite completing the arduous journey to Mecca, Vico said the money he had left was only enough to buy a flight ticket out of Saudi Arabia.

items.title: Australian woman assaulted in George Town cemetery, ex‑hotel guard jailed 12 years, caning
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 27 — A former hotel security guard was sentenced to 12 years’ imprisonment and 12 strokes of the cane by the Sessions Court here after admitting to raping and committing unnatural sex on an Australian woman last year. The court handed down concurrent sentences after the accused changed his plea to guilty earlier this week, Free Malaysia Today (FMT) reported. The accused was initially brought before the court on July 4 last year.

items.title: Dr Zaliha says former PKR members’ election bid under Bersama will not affect Pakatan campaign
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 27 — The presence of several former PKR members contesting under the Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) ticket in the Johor state election (PRN) will not affect grassroots support for Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidates, says Johor PKR chairman Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa. She said PKR members remained united in facing challenges from all parties contesting the 56 Johor state seats. Among the former Johor PKR members contesting under the Bersama ticket are Stanley Tan in the Stulang state seat, who previously served at the service centre of Johor Bahru Member of Parliament Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir, and Dr Zamil Najwah, PKR’s candidate for the Larkin state seat in the 2022 Johor election.

items.title: Ex-security guard gets 12 years' jail, rotan for raping Australian tourist
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A former hotel security guard was sentenced to 12 years' jail and 12 strokes of the rotan today for raping and sodomising an Australian tourist last year. Sessions Court judge Juraidah Abbas meted out the sentence after S. Prasanth, 25, pleaded guilty to both charges before the trial commenced. For the first charge of rape under Section 376(2)(b) of the Penal Code, Prasanth was sentenced to 12 years' jail and eight strokes of the rotan.

items.title: Anwar: Gazetting Bandar Malaysia land as Malay Reserve requires courage, consensus
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today described the decision to gazette 50 acres (20.23ha) of land in Bandar Malaysia as Malay Reserve Land as a significant move to safeguard Bumiputera interests, saying it required both political courage and careful consensus-building. He said the government reclaimed the 480-acre Bandar Malaysia site after the project stalled due to alleged abuses under the previous administration. The land is now managed by Khazanah Nasional Bhd and Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas).

items.title: Budi Diesel rollout smooth as early access phase begins
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Eligible Malaysians began enjoying fuel cost savings today as the early access phase of the Budi Madani Diesel (Budi Diesel) programme commenced, with the purchase of subsidised diesel at petrol stations across Peninsular Malaysia proceeding smoothly. Under the early access phase in Peninsular Malaysia, subsidised diesel is sold at RM2.15 per litre using MyKad verification at participating petrol stations. He said that as of 11am, nearly 10 eligible customers had successfully purchased subsidised diesel without any disruptions.

items.title: World's first anti-bullying tribunal: Madani govt's stand against bully culture
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Malaysia has made history by becoming the first nation in the world to set up an Anti-Bullying Tribunal, a dedicated and effective mechanism to tackle bullying head-on. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the tribunal's creation reflects the Madani government's resolve to ensure that bullying never takes root as a cultural norm in society. "No other country has such a tribunal.

items.title: PM to join Johor campaign trail, PH grand finale
items.summary: KULAI: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is expected to hit the campaign trail in Johor, with a grand finale Pakatan Harapan (PH) programme scheduled for July 9, just two days before Johoreans head to the polls. PH secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail confirmed the news, saying the prime minister will be lending his weight to the PH campaign. The PH grand finale on July 9 will also see all component party presidents in attendance, Saifuddin revealed.

items.title: 11-year-old killed after motorcycle being towed is hit by car
items.summary: JERTIH: An 11-year-old boy was killed after the motorcycle he was riding, which had broken down and was being towed by another motorcycle, was hit by a car along Jalan Kota Bharu-Kuala Terengganu near Kampung Bukit Kenak here late last night. Besut police chief Superintendent Mohd Rozaime Ab Rahman said the accident involved a Perodua Axia and a Modenas Dinamik motorcycle. "Initial investigations found that the victim's motorcycle had broken down and was being towed by another motorcycle at the time of the incident.

items.title: Mother dies shielding daughter in Venezuela quake
items.summary: CARACAS: A Venezuelan mother has died after saving her daughter during twin earthquakes that struck the country, according to footballer Héctor Bello and local media. Bello said on Instagram that his wife, named by Venezuelan outlets as Andrea, died protecting their toddler during the quakes. Bello said he travelled to Caracas, where his daughter and the child's aunt were receiving treatment in hospital.

items.title: A sanctuary I call home: A journalist's tribute to Malaysia's global standing
items.summary: LETTERS: Malaysia has been my home for over two decades—a land that welcomed me, shaped my professional journey, and embraced me as one of its own. Today, as I see Malaysia rise to the 12th spot in the Global Peace Index 2026, I am filled with a profound sense of pride that is both personal and professional. I wish to offer my deepest, most sincere tribute to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, and Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail.

items.title: Pakatan ditches ‘idealistic’ hundred-page manifestos for ‘realistic’ Johor vows; keeping MB choice under wraps
items.summary: PH secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the coalition’s manifesto has been finalised and is awaiting final endorsement by its top leadership at a meeting on June 29 before being launched, Harian Metro reported this afternoon. Saifuddin Nasution said this time the coalition wanted to present a manifesto that was “realistic, tangible and reasonable”, with a simpler presentation that would not dilute its policy offerings. On the question of PH's menteri besar candidate should the coalition win the Johor state election, Saifuddin Nasution said an announcement would only be made when the time was right.

items.title: Johor election: Full list of candidates for 56 seats
items.summary: Noraziah Mohd Razit | PH N.02 Jementah 1. Ng Kor Sim | PH (Incumbent) 3. Mohd Azhar Ibrahim | Barisan Nasional 2.

items.title: Johor polls: PH to fine-tune strategies for 14 straight fights
items.summary: KULAI: Pakatan Harapan (PH) will fine-tune its campaign strategies in 14 state seats that are headed for straight fights in the 16th Johor election. PH secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, who is also PKR's joint election director, said each seat would be analysed in detail as electoral trends in the country depend heavily on local factors. He noted that Perikatan Nasional's (PN) decision not to field candidates in all seats had contributed to the straight-fight scenarios.

items.title: Orang Asli party ASLI makes election debut with Endau seat bid in Johor polls
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 27 — Parti Orang Asli Malaysia (Asli) has made its first election appearance by fielding a candidate for the Endau state seat in the 16th Johor state election. The Endau seat, with 28,767 registered voters, will see a four-cornered fight involving Jati, Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate Alwiyah Talib, Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Saiful Nizam Samat and Perikatan Nasional (PN) candidate Hasnul Hakimi Hussien. Jati, 52, an Orang Asli from Endau, said ASLI’s participation aims to elevate the voice of the community, particularly in state leadership.

items.title: Body in suitcase: Australian tourist held over Thai teen's murder
items.summary: BANGKOK: An Australian has been arrested over the suspected murder of a 17-year-old Thai girl whose naked body was found stuffed inside a suitcase abandoned beside railway tracks in Pattaya. Police said no one else was seen entering the room after them. Australian media reported that surveillance footage later allegedly captured the Australian leaving the hotel alone while wheeling a large black suitcase.

items.title: State polls: Gobind warns against AI misuse, defends DAP's multiracial candidate lineup
items.summary: Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said he had repeatedly reminded political parties to exercise caution when using AI, stressing that all campaign materials must be authentic and based on accurate information. Also present were Deputy Finance Minister and DAP strategic director Liew Chin Tong, and Deputy Communications Minister and Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching. "The nomination process for all DAP and PH candidates went smoothly.

items.title: Authorities probe alleged Quranic verse error in influencer's book
items.summary: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan described the matter as "serious", as Putrajaya moves to strengthen the publishing ecosystem for religious books. "We consider this a serious matter involving negligence in the publication of works containing errors," Zulkifli said. Zulkifli said religious writers must ensure accuracy in their work.

items.title: Anwar urges Pakatan candidates to win Johor voters’ trust, prove civil politics can prevail
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 27 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today wished all PH candidates contesting the 16th Johor state election a successful campaign. Nomination for the 16th Johor state election concluded today, with 172 candidates confirmed to contest the 56 seats. PH and Barisan Nasional (BN) are fielding 56 candidates each, followed by Perikatan Nasional (PN) with 33, Parti Bersama Malaysia with 15, the Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (Muda) with four, Parti Orang Asli Malaysia (Asli) and Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM) with one candidate each, while six candidates are contesting as Independents.

items.title: 1 child or teen drowns every 3 days
items.summary: Beaches recorded 150 cases, followed by waterfalls and rapids (50), lakes and former mining pools (44), while 11 cases involved children drowning in drains. Sabah recorded the highest number of cases with 99, followed by Kelantan (95), Sarawak (82) and Johor (78). School holidays ranked third with 112 cases.

items.title: Anwar to join Johor election campaign with Pakatan grand finale two days before polling, says Saifuddin Nasution
items.summary: KULAI, June 27 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is expected to hit the campaign trail in Johor, with a grand finale Pakatan Harapan (PH) programme scheduled for July 9, just two days before Johoreans head to the polls. PH secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail confirmed the news, saying the Prime Minister will be lending his weight to the PH campaign. The PH grand finale on July 9 will also see all component party presidents in attendance, Saifuddin revealed.

items.title: ‘Competing in the daytime but friends at night’, Mat Sabu says Pakatan‑BN clash confined to Johor polls
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 27 — The contest between Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) in the Johor state election does not in any way disrupt the administration of the federal unity government, said Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu. He said that since the dissolution of the Johor State Legislative Assembly, relations and duties, particularly those involving Cabinet ministers from both parties, have continued smoothly. The Amanah president said that although both sides have their own strategies and calculations at the state level to win the election, this does not affect stability at the federal level.

items.title: Anwar says Bandar Malaysia Malay Reserve Land move requires political courage to protect Bumiputera interests
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 27 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the decision to gazette 50 acres (20.23 hectares) of land in Bandar Malaysia as Malay Reserve Land is a significant step to safeguard Bumiputera interests, requiring both political courage and careful consensus-building. He said the 480-acre Bandar Malaysia site was taken back by the government after the project failed due to alleged abuses under the previous administration, and is now being managed by Khazanah Nasional Bhd and Petroliam Nasional Bhd. “The 50 acres is equivalent to 5,000 acres in Kedah in terms of value.

items.title: DUKE highway crash: Police confirm two students burnt to death in BMW blaze, survivor hospitalised
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 27 — Two university students were burnt to death in a crash involving a car at the Jalan Kuching exit ramp on the Duta-Ulu Kelang Expressway (DUKE) here early this morning, while another passenger survived with minor injuries. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said preliminary investigations found that the BMW 328i Sport was travelling from Jalan Duta towards Selayang when it was believed to have been speeding before the driver lost control, skidded, and crashed into the left road divider, causing the vehicle to catch fire. He said the victims were two Malaysian students — the 22-year-old male driver, a student at Queensland University of Technology, Australia, and the 21-year-old male front passenger, a Sunway University student.

items.title: Four times a charm? Persistent independent Jeganathan braves Bukit Naning poll again
items.summary: MUAR: Three consecutive defeats have not dampened the spirit of independent candidate S. Jeganathan, who is once again trying his luck in Bukit Naning in the July 11 Johor election. Jeganathan previously contested as an independent in Bukit Naning in the 14th general election (GE14) and the 2022 Johor election, as well as the 2023 Simpang Jeram by-election, but lost on all three occasions. Asked about the issues he intended to focus on during the campaign, Jeganathan, a contractor, said he would prioritise infrastructure improvements, particularly repairing damaged roads in the Bukit Naning constituency.

items.title: Johor polls: Anwar calls on PH candidates to champion civil politics
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today wished all PH candidates contesting the 16th Johor state election a successful campaign. Nomination for the 16th Johor state election concluded today, with 172 candidates confirmed to contest the 56 seats. PH and Barisan Nasional (BN) are fielding 56 candidates each, followed by Perikatan Nasional (PN) with 33, Parti Bersama Malaysia with 15, the Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (Muda) with four, Parti Orang Asli Malaysia (Asli) and Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM) with one candidate each, while six candidates are contesting as Independents.

items.title: Govt rolls out RM60bil drive to strengthen micro, small and medium enterprises
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The government has planned more than 300 development programmes worth RM60 billion this year to help Malaysia's micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) boost growth. Sim said micro, small and medium enterprises remain the backbone of Malaysia's economy despite global challenges, including rising operating costs driven by supply chain disruptions and geopolitical uncertainty. He said government support for micro, small and medium enterprises had yielded positive results last year, with 344 entrepreneurship and development programmes worth RM31.63 billion helping to create 6,600 new entrepreneurs.

items.title: BN confident of winning back Bukit Kepong seat as Johor election campaign begin
items.summary: PAGOH, June 27 — Barisan Nasional (BN) has kicked off its campaign for the 16th Johor state election with high confidence in its bid to wrest back the Bukit Kepong seat from Perikatan Nasional (PN). Bukit Kepong, which falls under the Pagoh parliamentary constituency, has 37,683 eligible voters for this state election. The Johor state election will take place on July 11, with early voting on July 7.

items.title: Anwar announces RM1.5m allocation for Syariah legal aid, says reforms will not affect non-Muslims’ rights
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 27 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has announced a RM1.5 million allocation to strengthen access to Syariah legal aid services for the underprivileged, alongside renewed assurances that efforts to enhance the Syariah judicial system will not affect the rights of non-Muslims. He said the allocation to the Syariah Lawyers Association of Malaysia would be channelled through the Syariah Legal Aid Centre (BAGUS) to expand access to representation for those unable to afford Syariah legal services, particularly the poor and single mothers. “We have good laws, good courts, good lawyers and good judges, but access remains an issue.

items.title: In Malaysia, one in four rehab patients now battling addiction and mental illness
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS, June 27 — More than one in four drug abusers undergoing rehabilitation in Malaysia this year have also been diagnosed with mental health disorders, as the rise of synthetic drugs reshapes the country's addiction crisis. National Anti-Drug Agency (AADK) director-general Datuk Ruslin Jusoh said 1,346 people, or 27 per cent of the 5,030 substance abusers undergoing treatment at Narcotics Addiction Rehabilitation Centres between January and March this year were found to have what is known as a dual diagnosis, the New Straits Times reported today. Ruslin said the trend showed drug rehabilitation was no longer just about treating addiction, as an increasing number of patients were also struggling with mental illness.

items.title: PH to roll out manifesto focused on federal alignment, development
items.summary: KULAI: Pakatan Harapan will soon unveil its Johor election manifesto, focusing on ensuring the state is governed by a coalition aligned with the federal government to secure greater development and benefits. PH secretariat chief Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the draft manifesto had been finalised but remained subject to final endorsement during a meeting with the coalition's top leadership this Monday. He said PH would emphasise performance-based politics over identity politics, which he described as divisive.

items.title: Anwar to launch LRT3 Shah Alam Line tomorrow
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Light Rail Transit 3 (LRT3) Shah Alam Line, spanning 37.8 kilometres from Bandar Utama to Johan Setia, has been fully completed and is ready for passenger service. "The new stations are Bandar Utama, Kayu Ara, BU 11 (Bandar Utama 11), Damansara Idaman, Subang, Glenmarie 2, Kerjaya, Stadium Shah Alam, Dato' Menteri and UiTM Shah Alam. "The remaining 10 stations are Seksyen 7 Shah Alam, Bandar Baru Klang, Pasar Klang, Jalan Meru, Jambatan Kota, Taman Selatan, Seri Andalas, Klang Jaya, Bandar Bukit Tinggi and Johan Setia," he told reporters during a pre-opening media ride on the new service.

items.title: Five-cornered fights set to shake up Bukit Batu, Puteri Wangsa, Bukit Naning
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Three key Johor state seats are set for five-cornered contests, setting the stage for unpredictable outcomes in Bukit Batu, Puteri Wangsa and Bukit Naning as rival blocs vie for fragmented voter support. In Bukit Batu, incumbent Pakatan Harapan assemblyman Arthur Chiong Sen Sern will defend his seat against Barisan Nasional's R. Kumaran, Muda's M. Premanand, Bersama's G. Tamili and independent Datuk Kamaruzaman Ali. In Puteri Wangsa, PH's Dr Maszlee Malik will face BN's Teow Chia Ling, Muda's Rashifa Aljunied, Bersama's Nicholas Paul Vincent and independent Wang Wee Siong.

items.title: Malaysia's biggest tech challenge is talent, not infrastructure, says Mosti
items.summary: Ministry of Science, Technology and Innovation’s (MOSTI) deputy secretary-general (Planning and Science Enculturation) Datuk Ruziah Shafie said that developing a stronger, more resilient, and globally competitive future workforce requires early exposure to science, technology, and innovation (STI), as well as science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM), through continuous science culture and outreach programmes. She said that the National Science Week (MSN) programme is one of MOSTI’s key initiatives to cultivate youth’s interest in these fields while also bridging the gap between classroom learning and the real needs of high-technology industries. Malaysia can no longer remain merely a consumer of technology; we must rise to become a creator of technology.

items.title: 9 arrested in illegal bauxite mining raid, RM3.8mil haul seized in Kuantan
items.summary: KUANTAN: Authorities arrested nine people and seized about 10,000 tonnes of bauxite-bearing soil, heavy machinery and lorries worth a combined RM3.75 million in a raid on an illegal mining operation at a Felda plantation in Bukit Goh. According to Harian Metro, the raid at 11.30am on June 25 under Op Bersepadu Khazanah involved the General Operations Force (GOF) Battalion 7 Intelligence Branch and the State Enforcement Unit. Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the seizure included soil suspected to contain bauxite worth RM2 million, two excavators valued at RM1 million and three lorries worth RM750,000.

items.title: Police tracking individual believed to have been with missing Nor Afiza
items.summary: TAIPING: Police are tracking an individual believed to have been with 41-year-old Nor Afiza Zainuddin before her disappearance 11 days ago. Acting Taiping police chief Superintendent Mohd Ariffridzuan Ezahar said the search for the mother of two was ongoing, with investigators pursuing fresh leads. Nor Afiza was reported missing on June 18.

items.title: Prescription for trouble: Unlicensed doctor at Subang Bestari clinic nabbed after eight months of cut-price consultations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — A Pakistani man suspected of posing as an unlicensed doctor has been detained after Immigration officers raided a private clinic in Subang Bestari, Selangor, uncovering what authorities believe was an illegally operated medical practice that had been running for eight months. The suspect is believed to have been working as an “unlicensed doctor” despite not possessing any valid pass or travel documents to remain in Malaysia, the Immigration Department said on Facebook today. The private clinic was allegedly run by a foreign national who did not have legal authorisation to work in Malaysia’s medical sector, the department said.

items.title: Johor polls: Maszlee pledges balanced development across all districts
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 27 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Puteri Wangsa state constituency, Dr Maszlee Malik, is determined to bring balanced development across all districts in Johor if given the mandate in the upcoming polls. He said a PH victory in Puteri Wangsa, and across the state, is crucial to usher in a new chapter of development that is not concentrated solely in southern Johor, but spread evenly so that every segment of society can enjoy a better quality of life. “We want Johor to progress in a balanced way across all its districts, not just the southern part.

items.title: Johor polls: Zahid pledges ‘mature politics’, says BN won’t attack rivals
items.summary: PONTIAN, June 27 — BN will adopt a ‘mature politics’ approach by refraining from attacking, criticising or disparaging its rivals throughout the campaign for the 16th Johor state election. BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the approach is important to preserve political harmony, while allowing the coalition’s campaign machinery to focus on promoting the strengths of its candidates courteously and constructively. In the 2022 Johor state election, BN secured a two-thirds majority after winning 40 of the 56 DUN seats to form the state government.

items.title: PM: Malaysia must accelerate adoption of drone technology
items.summary: SEPANG: Malaysia must accelerate its adoption and development of new technologies, particularly in the Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) or drone industry, to bolster its global economic standing, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said the drone industry, which intersects with artificial intelligence (AI) and digital transformation, has emerged as a powerful catalyst for innovation, productivity and economic growth. Co-organised by the World UAV Federation (WUAVF) Malaysia Chapter and the Malaysia UAV Development Association (MUDA), the three-day expo surpassed expectations by featuring more than 100 exhibition booths and attracting thousands of visitors and industry delegates from 46 countries.

items.title: Police net two suspects, seize suspected heroin after Jalan Kuching pursuit that led to pile-up
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — Two men suspected of transporting heroin were arrested after a high-speed police chase ended in a multi-vehicle collision on Jalan Kuching, with officers recovering nearly 20g of suspected drugs from their car. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the suspects, aged 40 and 42, have been remanded to assist investigations, the New Straits Times reported this afternoon. Fadil said police instructed the driver to stop, but the vehicle instead sped off, triggering a pursuit towards Jalan Kuching.

items.title: Johor polls: 172 candidates confirmed after nomination closes
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 27 — The Election Commission (EC) has confirmed that 172 candidates from various political parties will contest the 16th Johor state election after the nomination process concluded at all 56 nomination centres today. Ramlan said 14 seats would see straight fights, with 27 featuring three-cornered contests, 12 involving four candidates and three drawing five candidates. He said the campaign period officially begins today and will run until 11.59 pm on July 10, with 56 Election Campaign Enforcement Teams (PP-KPR) deployed to monitor all campaign activities.

items.title: In Pagoh, Muhyiddin shrugs off PAS snub, vows Bersatu machinery will drive Johor fight
items.summary: PAGOH, June 27 — Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) founding president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin said he respected PAS’ decision not to assist the party’s campaign in the 16th Johor state election, stressing that Bersatu would press ahead on the strength of its own machinery. He said he believed the decision would not affect Bersatu’s campaign, which remained focused on the party’s policies and track record in winning public support. Muhyiddin said Bersatu would nevertheless remain open to assisting its coalition partners if its support was needed during the Johor election campaign.

items.title: PM Anwar to launch LRT3 Shah Alam Line tomorrow, 20 new stations set to transform commutes
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 27 — The Light Rail Transit 3 (LRT3) Shah Alam Line, spanning 37.8 kilometres from Bandar Utama to Johan Setia, has been fully completed and is now ready for passenger service. “The new stations are Bandar Utama, Kayu Ara, BU 11 (Bandar Utama 11), Damansara Idaman, Subang, Glenmarie 2, Kerjaya, Stadium Shah Alam, Dato’ Menteri, and UiTM Shah Alam. “The remaining 10 stations are Seksyen 7 Shah Alam, Bandar Baru Klang, Pasar Klang, Jalan Meru, Jambatan Kota, Taman Selatan, Seri Andalas, Klang Jaya, Bandar Bukit Tinggi, and Johan Setia,” he told reporters during a recent pre-opening media ride on the new service.

items.title: Tuanku Muhriz installs new Undangs of Sungei Ujong and Rembau, calls for loyalty
items.summary: KUALA PILAH, June 27 — The Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, today formalised the installation of two new Undangs (ruling chieftains) in separate ceremonies at Istana Besar Seri Menanti, underscoring the continued importance of adat and consensus in the state’s governance structure. In the morning ceremony, Muhammad Faris Johari, 28, was installed as the 11th Undang of Luak Sungei Ujong, bearing the title Datuk Klana Petra, while in the afternoon, Hassan Ab Hamid, 67, was installed as the 22nd Undang of Luak Rembau, with the title Datuk Sedia Raja. Also in attendance were Tunku Ampuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Aishah Rohani Tengku Besar Mahmud, Tunku Besar Seri Menanti Tunku Ali Redhauddin Tuanku Muhriz, and Tunku Panglima Besar Tunku Zain Al-‘Abidin.

items.title: Malaysia to seize drone boom, PM Anwar promises stronger rules, research and industry collaboration
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 27 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today pledged government backing to build Malaysia’s unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) industry into a new engine of economic growth, saying Putrajaya will strengthen regulations, research and collaboration to accelerate the sector’s development. Speaking at the closing ceremony of the country’s first drone exhibition today, Anwar said the government has instructed ministries, research institutions and relevant agencies to provide the support needed to develop the local UAV ecosystem as drone technology becomes increasingly important for both commercial and defence applications. Citing the growing adoption of drones alongside artificial intelligence and digital technologies, Anwar said Malaysia must move quickly to seize opportunities in what is expected to become a rapidly expanding global market.

items.title: Bukit Batu contest heats up, five candidates challenge Pakatan incumbent in high‑stakes battle
items.summary: KULAI, June 27 — The Bukit Batu state constituency is set to see a five-way contest, turning the once quiet seat into a ‘hot seat’ for the upcoming Johor state election. The contest for Bukit Batu will see incumbent Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Arthur Chiong Sen Sern defend his seat from Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate RK Kumaran; Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (Muda) candidate, M. Premanand; Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) candidate, G. Tamili and independent hopeful Datuk Kamaruzaman Ali. In the last state election in 2022, Chiong won by a razor-thin majority of 137 votes with a total of 9,439 votes in a four-cornered contest for the Bukit Batu state seat.

items.title: King urges modernisation of Malaysian Armed Forces with technology-driven defence assets
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 27 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today called for a strategic transformation of the Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) in line with the ‘Future Force’ concept to build an integrated military capable of meeting the challenges of fifth-generation warfare. “I therefore call on the government to prioritise the acquisition of modern, technology-driven defence assets and support the new structure of the Malaysian Armed Forces,” His Majesty said. Sultan Ibrahim said this at the Royal Commissioning Parade and Presentation of Commissioning Scrolls to Malaysian Armed Forces Officer Cadets 2026 at Dataran Panglima Tentera Darat, Kem Perdana Sungai Besi, here today.—MORE Also present were Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin and Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) chief Gen Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman.

items.title: Former health minister Dr Adham Baba makes political comeback in three-way Pasir Raja contest
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, June 27 — Former Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Adham Baba will make his electoral comeback in a three-cornered contest for the Pasir Raja seat in the 16th Johor state election. Polling for the Johor state election will be held on July 11, with early voting scheduled for July 7. For the latest updates on the Johor state election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor/.

items.title: MOH website hacked, ministry warns Malaysians avoid site and rely on verified platforms
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 27 — Malaysia’s Health Ministry has confirmed its official website was hacked after the portal displayed a message believed to have been left by the attackers. The incident forced the site offline and sparked urgent recovery efforts. “The ministry takes this incident seriously and is working closely with relevant agencies to strengthen system security and prevent a recurrence,” it said.

items.title: Police: Johor nomination day concluded peacefully with 4,832 personnel deployed
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 27 — The nomination process for the 16th Johor state election at all 56 nomination centres proceeded smoothly today without any untoward incidents. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said police had not received any reports of incidents that could disrupt the process. He said 4,832 police officers and personnel were deployed to ensure the nomination process ran smoothly.

items.title: Two-term incumbent Ee Chin Li faces BN challenger How Chin Teck in Tangkak race
items.summary: TANGKAK, June 27 — The Tangkak state constituency will see a straight fight between candidates from Barisan Nasional (BN) and Pakatan Harapan (PH) in the Johor state election, scheduled for July 11. The contest was announced by Tangkak election returning officer Ungku Suzana Ungku Mohammad at the nomination centre, Dewan Jubli Intan Sultan Ibrahim here, after the nomination process closed at 10 am. The Election Commission (EC) has set polling day for July 11, with early voting scheduled for July 7.
